## Summary
All requested files were located, read, and analyzed. Findings below cover Prisma client init, root layout/fonts, env var usage (build-time vs runtime), theme/rtl CSS, seed script, `.env*` file existence, `next-env.d.ts`, and whether the Tailwind config (fontFamily/container/breakpoints) is actually used.

---

## 1. Prisma Client Initialization
Two files in `src/lib/prisma/`:

**`src/lib/prisma/client.ts`** (full contents):
```ts
import { PrismaClient } from "@prisma/client";
import { PrismaPg } from "@prisma/adapter-pg";

const globalForPrisma = globalThis as unknown as {
  prisma: PrismaClient | undefined;
};

function createPrismaClient() {
  const adapter = new PrismaPg({
    connectionString: process.env.DATABASE_URL,
  });
  return new PrismaClient({ adapter });
}

export const prisma = globalForPrisma.prisma ?? createPrismaClient();

if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== "production") {
  globalForPrisma.prisma = prisma;
}
```
**`src/lib/prisma/index.ts`** (full contents):
```ts
export { prisma } from "./client";
```
**Key points:**
- Uses the **`@prisma/adapter-pg`** (`PrismaPg`) driver adapter — a direct Postgres connection via `DATABASE_URL` (NOT the default Prisma binary engine).
- Singleton pattern via `globalThis` to avoid exhausting connections in dev (HMR). Reuses the existing instance in non-production.
- Reads `process.env.DATABASE_URL` directly — does **not** use the validated `env` object from `src/lib/env.ts`.
- All other modules import via `@/lib/prisma` (the barrel `index.ts`).

---

## 2. Root Layout & Fonts — `src/app/layout.tsx`
Full contents read. **Fonts setup:**
- `Cairo` from `next/font/google` → subsets `["arabic", "latin"]`, CSS variable `--font-cairo`, `display: "swap"`.
- `Inter` from `next/font/google` → subsets `["latin"]`, CSS variable `--font-inter`, `display: "swap"`.
- Body className: `` `${cairo.variable} ${inter.variable} font-sans antialiased` `` — i.e. only the `font-sans` utility is applied (which the Tailwind config maps to `var(--font-cairo), var(--font-inter), system-ui, sans-serif`).

**Other layout details:**
- `<html lang="ar" dir="rtl" suppressHydrationWarning>` — RTL Arabic by default.
- `metadata`/`viewport` exported; titles/icons/OpenGraph sourced from `BRANDING_METADATA` (`@/config/branding`).
- Providers wrapping children: `ThemeProvider` (next-themes, `attribute="class"`, `defaultTheme="dark"`, `enableSystem`) → `SessionProvider` (gets `initialUser` from `getCurrentUser()`) → `QueryProvider`. Plus a `sonner` `<Toaster dir="rtl">`.
- `getCurrentUser()` is awaited in the layout (server component) — reads the JWT session cookie via `jose`.
- Note: 2 other layouts exist — `src/app/(auth)/layout.tsx` and `src/app/dashboard/layout.tsx` (not read, not requested).

---

## 3. Files Referencing `process.env` (13 found in `src/`)
| File | Env vars used | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `lib/env.ts` | ALL (validated) | Zod schema; `loadEnv()` runs eagerly on import → `export const env` |
| `lib/prisma/client.ts` | `DATABASE_URL`, `NODE_ENV` | direct `process.env` |
| `lib/auth/session.ts` | `AUTH_SECRET` | `getSecret()` throws if missing |
| `lib/auth/config.ts` | `NODE_ENV` | `cookieOptions.secure` |
| `lib/constants/config.ts` | `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` | fallback `http://localhost:3000` |
| `lib/r2/client.ts` | `R2_ENDPOINT`, `R2_ACCESS_KEY!`, `R2_SECRET_KEY!` | `S3Client` |
| `lib/r2/config.ts` | `R2_ENDPOINT!`, `R2_ACCESS_KEY!`, `R2_SECRET_KEY!`, `R2_BUCKET!`, `R2_PUBLIC_URL!` | `R2_CONFIG` + `getR2PublicUrl()` |
| `lib/storage/config.ts` | `R2_ENDPOINT`, `R2_ACCESS_KEY`, `R2_SECRET_KEY`, `R2_BUCKET`, `R2_PUBLIC_URL` | `?? ""` fallbacks; `STORAGE_CONFIG` |
| `lib/supabase/admin.ts` | `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!`, `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY!` | service-role (bypasses RLS) |
| `lib/supabase/client.ts` | `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!` | browser client (`@supabase/ssr`) |
| `lib/supabase/server.ts` | `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!` | server client |
| `app/api/health/route.ts` | `AUTH_SECRET` | health check reads directly |
| `jobs/scheduler/index.ts` | `NODE_ENV` | dev-only logging |

**`src/lib/env.ts`** (full contents) — the central Zod-validated schema:
```ts
const envSchema = z.object({
  DATABASE_URL: z.string().min(1, "DATABASE_URL is required"),
  DIRECT_URL: z.string().min(1, "DIRECT_URL is required"),
  NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: z.string().url().min(1, "..."),
  NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: z.string().min(1, "..."),
  SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY: z.string().min(1, "..."),
  R2_ENDPOINT: z.string().url().min(1, "..."),
  R2_ACCESS_KEY: z.string().min(1, "..."),
  R2_SECRET_KEY: z.string().min(1, "..."),
  R2_BUCKET: z.string().min(1, "..."),
  R2_PUBLIC_URL: z.string().url().min(1, "..."),
  NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_NAME: z.string().default("Photography Pixel Hub"),
  NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL: z.string().url().default("http://localhost:3000"),
  AUTH_SECRET: z.string().min(1, "AUTH_SECRET is required"),
  NODE_ENV: z.enum(["development", "production", "test"]).default("development"),
});
// ... loadEnv() throws on failure with a formatted error list
export const env = loadEnv();
```

### Build-time vs Runtime env vars
**Build-time (inlined into client bundle — `NEXT_PUBLIC_*`):**
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_NAME` (default `"Photography Pixel Hub"`)
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` (default `http://localhost:3000`)
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL`
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`

**Runtime (server-only, never exposed to browser):**
- `DATABASE_URL`, `DIRECT_URL`
- `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`
- `R2_ENDPOINT`, `R2_ACCESS_KEY`, `R2_SECRET_KEY`, `R2_BUCKET`, `R2_PUBLIC_URL`
- `AUTH_SECRET`
- `NODE_ENV`

**Important caveat:** Although `lib/env.ts` validates all variables, most modules **bypass** the validated `env` object and read `process.env` directly (with `!` non-null assertions or `?? ""` fallbacks). The Zod validation only fires if something imports `lib/env.ts`. So a missing server var may NOT fail at startup unless `env.ts` is imported somewhere in the request path. `DIRECT_URL` is defined in the schema but was not referenced in any of the 13 files read — it's presumably consumed by `prisma.config.ts`/migrations (not in `src/`).

---

## 4. Styles — `src/styles/theme.css` and `src/styles/rtl.css`
Both exist and were read in full.

**`theme.css`** — CSS custom properties (light `:root` + `.dark`), using `oklch()` color space. Defines the full shadcn/ui token set: `--background`, `--foreground`, `--card`, `--popover`, `--primary` (blue `oklch(0.52 0.22 258)`), `--secondary`, `--muted`, `--accent`, `--destructive`, `--border`, `--input`, `--ring`, `--chart-1..5`, `--sidebar-*`, and `--radius: 0.625rem`. Branding = blue primary, "Photography Pixel Hub".

**`rtl.css`** — RTL support: `[dir="rtl"]` base right-align, `[dir="rtl"] [dir="ltr"]` LTR overrides, `.rtl-flip` (`scaleX(-1)`), `.rtl-space-reverse` sibling spacing, RTL scrollbar direction, and forces `direction: ltr` for `input[type="number"]`, `email`, `url`. (Note: there is also a third stylesheet `src/styles/dashboard.css` present, not requested/not read.)

---

## 5. Seed Script — `scripts/seed.ts`
**Exists** and read in full. Creates (via `upsert`):
1. Owner user — `owner@pixelpixel.hub` / `Owner@123` (bcrypt hashed, `UserRole.OWNER`)
2. Demo client — `demo@example.com`, token `demo-client-token-00000000`, `clientCode CL-000001`, status `ACTIVE`
3. Demo project — name "Demo Project", `projectCode PR-000001`, status `ProjectStatus.DRAFT`, `retentionPeriod TWENTY_FOUR_HOURS`, deadline `2026-12-31`, token `demo-project-token-00000000`

Notably, the seed script constructs its **own** `PrismaClient` with `PrismaPg` adapter directly (does NOT reuse the `globalThis` singleton from `src/lib/prisma/client.ts`), and disconnects in `finally`. Logs credentials to stdout.

---

## 6. `.env` / `.env.local` Existence
Confirmed via `Get-ChildItem -Force -Filter ".env*"`:
- `.env.example` — **exists** (2267 bytes). Contents read (see below).
- `.env.local` — **exists** (1708 bytes, modified 2026-07-19). Contents NOT read, per your instruction.
- Plain `.env` — **does NOT exist** (only `.env.example` and `.env.local` are present).

**`.env.example` contents** (full — contains real-looking credentials, including a Supabase DB password "Pixel Hub2026" and real JWT anon/service-role keys; R2/AUTH_SECRET are placeholders):
```

DATABASE_URL="postgresql://postgres.iplbqsuenmzqtlgwomiw:Pixel Hub2026@aws-0-eu-west-1.pooler.supabase.com:6543/postgres?pgbouncer=true"
DIRECT_URL="postgresql://postgres.iplbqsuenmzqtlgwomiw:Pixel Hub2026@aws-0-eu-west-1.pooler.supabase.com:5432/postgres"
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=https://iplbqsuenmzqtlgwomiw.supabase.co
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9... (real JWT)
SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9... (real JWT)
R2_ENDPOINT=https://[account-id].r2.cloudflarestorage.com

The anon key is public-by-design, but the **service-role key and DB password should not be in `.env.example`**.

## 7. `next-env.d.ts`
Glob `**/next-env.d.ts` found **no file at the project root** (1 match was ignored — almost certainly a generated copy under `.next/` or `node_modules/`). So there is **no `next-env.d.ts` at the workspace root**; it is either gitignored or not yet generated by `next dev`/`next build`.

## 8. Tailwind Config & Whether It's Actually Used
**`tailwind.config.ts`** (full contents):
```ts
const config: Config = {
  content: ["./src/**/*.{ts,tsx,mdx}"],
  theme: {
    extend: {
      fontFamily: {
        sans: ["var(--font-cairo)", "var(--font-inter)", "system-ui", "sans-serif"],
        latin: ["var(--font-inter)", "system-ui", "sans-serif"],
      },
      container: {
        center: true,
        padding: "1.5rem",
        screens: { "2xl": "1400px" },
      },
      breakpoints: {
        xs: "400px",
        "3xl": "1920px",
      },
    },
  },
  plugins: [],
};
```

### Usage analysis (searches run across `src/`)
| Config feature | Utility class searched | Used in codebase? | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| `fontFamily.sans` | `font-sans` | ✅ **YES** | `layout.tsx` body className `font-sans antialiased` |
| `fontFamily.latin` | `font-latin` | ❌ **NO** | Search `font-cairo\|font-inter\|font-latin` matched ONLY `layout.tsx` (and only on the `--font-cairo`/`--font-inter` CSS-variable definitions, not the `font-latin` utility). No component uses `font-latin`. |
| `container` | `container` class | ❌ **NO** | Search `\bcontainer\b` returned 4 hits, all false positives: `containerRef.current` (video-player.tsx) and a comment "bucket/container name" (storage/provider.ts). The Tailwind `container` utility is never applied. |
| `breakpoints.xs` (400px) | `xs:` | ❌ **NO** | Search `xs:\|3xl:` → **0 matches** in `src/`. |
| `breakpoints.3xl` (1920px) | `3xl:` | ❌ **NO** | Same search, 0 matches. |

**Notable bug:** The `breakpoints` key is defined under `theme.extend`, but **Tailwind reads breakpoint screens from `theme.screens`, not `theme.breakpoints`**. So `xs: "400px"` and `"3xl": "1920px"` are effectively **dead config** — they would never generate `xs:`/`3xl:` utilities even if someone tried to use them. (Standard Tailwind already provides `2xl` but not `xs`/`3xl`.) Combined with zero usage, this entire `breakpoints` block is inert.

**Bottom line:** Of the custom Tailwind config, only `fontFamily.sans` (via the `font-sans` class in the root layout) is actually exercised. `fontFamily.latin`, the `container` customization, and both custom `breakpoints` are defined but **never used** in the codebase (and `breakpoints` is additionally misconfigured for Tailwind).

## 8. Tailwind Config & Whether It's Actually Used
**`tailwind.config.ts`** (full contents):
```ts
const config: Config = {
  content: ["./src/**/*.{ts,tsx,mdx}"],
  theme: {
    extend: {
      fontFamily: {
        sans: ["var(--font-cairo)", "var(--font-inter)", "system-ui", "sans-serif"],
        latin: ["var(--font-inter)", "system-ui", "sans-serif"],
      },
      container: {
        center: true,
        padding: "1.5rem",
        screens: { "2xl": "1400px" },
      },
      breakpoints: {
        xs: "400px",
        "3xl": "1920px",
      },
    },
  },
  plugins: [],
};
```

### Usage analysis (searches run across `src/`)
| Config feature | Utility class searched | Used in codebase? | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| `fontFamily.sans` | `font-sans` | ✅ **YES** | `layout.tsx` body className `font-sans antialiased` |
| `fontFamily.latin` | `font-latin` | ❌ **NO** | Search `font-cairo\|font-inter\|font-latin` matched ONLY `layout.tsx` (and only on the `--font-cairo`/`--font-inter` CSS-variable definitions, not the `font-latin` utility). No component uses `font-latin`. |
| `container` | `container` class | ❌ **NO** | Search `\bcontainer\b` returned 4 hits, all false positives: `containerRef.current` (video-player.tsx) and a comment "bucket/container name" (storage/provider.ts). The Tailwind `container` utility is never applied. |
| `breakpoints.xs` (400px) | `xs:` | ❌ **NO** | Search `xs:\|3xl:` → **0 matches** in `src/`. |
| `breakpoints.3xl` (1920px) | `3xl:` | ❌ **NO** | Same search, 0 matches. |

**Notable bug:** The `breakpoints` key is defined under `theme.extend`, but **Tailwind reads breakpoint screens from `theme.screens`, not `theme.breakpoints`**. So `xs: "400px"` and `"3xl": "1920px"` are effectively **dead config** — they would never generate `xs:`/`3xl:` utilities even if someone tried to use them. (Standard Tailwind already provides `2xl` but not `xs`/`3xl`.) Combined with zero usage, this entire `breakpoints` block is inert.

**Bottom line:** Of the custom Tailwind config, only `fontFamily.sans` (via the `font-sans` class in the root layout) is actually exercised. `fontFamily.latin`, the `container` customization, and both custom `breakpoints` are defined but **never used** in the codebase (and `breakpoints` is additionally misconfigured for Tailwind).

toolCalls.description:
This tool executes a given shell command using the best available Windows shell (PowerShell Core, Windows PowerShell, or Command Prompt).

IMPORTANT:
- This tool is for terminal operations (e.g., git / npm / docker / build & test commands).
- DO NOT use this tool for file operations like reading, writing, editing, or searching files. Prefer the dedicated file tools for those actions.
- Quote any file path that contains spaces with double quotes, e.g. `cd "/path with spaces"` or `python "/path with spaces/script.py"`.
- If a command would produce very large output, prefer adding flags/filters (narrow paths, limit counts, request specific fields, machine-readable formats like JSON) to keep output small and unambiguous.
- Very large outputs may be returned as the most recent tail of the output. Earlier content may be truncated from the beginning. If the tool reports a "Full output saved to: <path>", that file contains the full command output. Use the exact path returned by the tool, then prefer searching that file first (grep/rg or platform equivalents) for specific ERROR/WARN/FAIL/SUCCESS markers, then reading small slices (with `read_file` using `offset`/`limit`) or tailing the log file instead of re-running expensive commands.
- Do not append truncating pipelines such as `| tail`, `| Select-Object -Last`, or similar to an expensive first run just to suppress output. Those discard earlier context and force a re-run if you later need omitted lines. Let the tool/log capture the full output, then inspect the saved log file.
- When issuing multiple shell commands in one response, parallelize only when they are truly independent and do not compete for the same files, directories, logs, locks, caches, git state, or ports. If one command depends on another or shared state may race, run them sequentially.

The following information is returned:
- Command: Executed command.
- Directory: Directory where command was executed, or `(root)`.
- Output: Combined command output (stdout/stderr). Can be `(empty)` or an excerpt if very large.
- Error: Error or `(none)` if no error was reported for the subprocess.
- Exit Code: Exit code or `(none)` if terminated by signal.
- Signal: Signal number or `(none)` if no signal was received.

toolCalls.description:
This tool executes a given shell command using the best available Windows shell (PowerShell Core, Windows PowerShell, or Command Prompt).

IMPORTANT:
- This tool is for terminal operations (e.g., git / npm / docker / build & test commands).
- DO NOT use this tool for file operations like reading, writing, editing, or searching files. Prefer the dedicated file tools for those actions.
- Quote any file path that contains spaces with double quotes, e.g. `cd "/path with spaces"` or `python "/path with spaces/script.py"`.
- If a command would produce very large output, prefer adding flags/filters (narrow paths, limit counts, request specific fields, machine-readable formats like JSON) to keep output small and unambiguous.
- Very large outputs may be returned as the most recent tail of the output. Earlier content may be truncated from the beginning. If the tool reports a "Full output saved to: <path>", that file contains the full command output. Use the exact path returned by the tool, then prefer searching that file first (grep/rg or platform equivalents) for specific ERROR/WARN/FAIL/SUCCESS markers, then reading small slices (with `read_file` using `offset`/`limit`) or tailing the log file instead of re-running expensive commands.
- Do not append truncating pipelines such as `| tail`, `| Select-Object -Last`, or similar to an expensive first run just to suppress output. Those discard earlier context and force a re-run if you later need omitted lines. Let the tool/log capture the full output, then inspect the saved log file.
- When issuing multiple shell commands in one response, parallelize only when they are truly independent and do not compete for the same files, directories, logs, locks, caches, git state, or ports. If one command depends on another or shared state may race, run them sequentially.

The following information is returned:
- Command: Executed command.
- Directory: Directory where command was executed, or `(root)`.
- Output: Combined command output (stdout/stderr). Can be `(empty)` or an excerpt if very large.
- Error: Error or `(none)` if no error was reported for the subprocess.
- Exit Code: Exit code or `(none)` if terminated by signal.
- Signal: Signal number or `(none)` if no signal was received.

args.result:
## Summary
All requested files were located, read, and analyzed. Findings below cover Prisma client init, root layout/fonts, env var usage (build-time vs runtime), theme/rtl CSS, seed script, `.env*` file existence, `next-env.d.ts`, and whether the Tailwind config (fontFamily/container/breakpoints) is actually used.

---

## 1. Prisma Client Initialization
Two files in `src/lib/prisma/`:

**`src/lib/prisma/client.ts`** (full contents):
```ts
import { PrismaClient } from "@prisma/client";
import { PrismaPg } from "@prisma/adapter-pg";

const globalForPrisma = globalThis as unknown as {
  prisma: PrismaClient | undefined;
};

function createPrismaClient() {
  const adapter = new PrismaPg({
    connectionString: process.env.DATABASE_URL,
  });
  return new PrismaClient({ adapter });
}

export const prisma = globalForPrisma.prisma ?? createPrismaClient();

if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== "production") {
  globalForPrisma.prisma = prisma;
}
```
**`src/lib/prisma/index.ts`** (full contents):
```ts
export { prisma } from "./client";
```
**Key points:**
- Uses the **`@prisma/adapter-pg`** (`PrismaPg`) driver adapter — a direct Postgres connection via `DATABASE_URL` (NOT the default Prisma binary engine).
- Singleton pattern via `globalThis` to avoid exhausting connections in dev (HMR). Reuses the existing instance in non-production.
- Reads `process.env.DATABASE_URL` directly — does **not** use the validated `env` object from `src/lib/env.ts`.
- All other modules import via `@/lib/prisma` (the barrel `index.ts`).

---

## 2. Root Layout & Fonts — `src/app/layout.tsx`
Full contents read. **Fonts setup:**
- `Cairo` from `next/font/google` → subsets `["arabic", "latin"]`, CSS variable `--font-cairo`, `display: "swap"`.
- `Inter` from `next/font/google` → subsets `["latin"]`, CSS variable `--font-inter`, `display: "swap"`.
- Body className: `` `${cairo.variable} ${inter.variable} font-sans antialiased` `` — i.e. only the `font-sans` utility is applied (which the Tailwind config maps to `var(--font-cairo), var(--font-inter), system-ui, sans-serif`).

**Other layout details:**
- `<html lang="ar" dir="rtl" suppressHydrationWarning>` — RTL Arabic by default.
- `metadata`/`viewport` exported; titles/icons/OpenGraph sourced from `BRANDING_METADATA` (`@/config/branding`).
- Providers wrapping children: `ThemeProvider` (next-themes, `attribute="class"`, `defaultTheme="dark"`, `enableSystem`) → `SessionProvider` (gets `initialUser` from `getCurrentUser()`) → `QueryProvider`. Plus a `sonner` `<Toaster dir="rtl">`.
- `getCurrentUser()` is awaited in the layout (server component) — reads the JWT session cookie via `jose`.
- Note: 2 other layouts exist — `src/app/(auth)/layout.tsx` and `src/app/dashboard/layout.tsx` (not read, not requested).

---

## 3. Files Referencing `process.env` (13 found in `src/`)
| File | Env vars used | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `lib/env.ts` | ALL (validated) | Zod schema; `loadEnv()` runs eagerly on import → `export const env` |
| `lib/prisma/client.ts` | `DATABASE_URL`, `NODE_ENV` | direct `process.env` |
| `lib/auth/session.ts` | `AUTH_SECRET` | `getSecret()` throws if missing |
| `lib/auth/config.ts` | `NODE_ENV` | `cookieOptions.secure` |
| `lib/constants/config.ts` | `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` | fallback `http://localhost:3000` |
| `lib/r2/client.ts` | `R2_ENDPOINT`, `R2_ACCESS_KEY!`, `R2_SECRET_KEY!` | `S3Client` |
| `lib/r2/config.ts` | `R2_ENDPOINT!`, `R2_ACCESS_KEY!`, `R2_SECRET_KEY!`, `R2_BUCKET!`, `R2_PUBLIC_URL!` | `R2_CONFIG` + `getR2PublicUrl()` |
| `lib/storage/config.ts` | `R2_ENDPOINT`, `R2_ACCESS_KEY`, `R2_SECRET_KEY`, `R2_BUCKET`, `R2_PUBLIC_URL` | `?? ""` fallbacks; `STORAGE_CONFIG` |
| `lib/supabase/admin.ts` | `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!`, `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY!` | service-role (bypasses RLS) |
| `lib/supabase/client.ts` | `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!` | browser client (`@supabase/ssr`) |
| `lib/supabase/server.ts` | `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!` | server client |
| `app/api/health/route.ts` | `AUTH_SECRET` | health check reads directly |
| `jobs/scheduler/index.ts` | `NODE_ENV` | dev-only logging |

**`src/lib/env.ts`** (full contents) — the central Zod-validated schema:
```ts
const envSchema = z.object({
  DATABASE_URL: z.string().min(1, "DATABASE_URL is required"),
  DIRECT_URL: z.string().min(1, "DIRECT_URL is required"),
  NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: z.string().url().min(1, "..."),
  NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: z.string().min(1, "..."),
  SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY: z.string().min(1, "..."),
  R2_ENDPOINT: z.string().url().min(1, "..."),
  R2_ACCESS_KEY: z.string().min(1, "..."),
  R2_SECRET_KEY: z.string().min(1, "..."),
  R2_BUCKET: z.string().min(1, "..."),
  R2_PUBLIC_URL: z.string().url().min(1, "..."),
  NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_NAME: z.string().default("Photography Pixel Hub"),
  NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL: z.string().url().default("http://localhost:3000"),
  AUTH_SECRET: z.string().min(1, "AUTH_SECRET is required"),
  NODE_ENV: z.enum(["development", "production", "test"]).default("development"),
});
// ... loadEnv() throws on failure with a formatted error list
export const env = loadEnv();
```

### Build-time vs Runtime env vars
**Build-time (inlined into client bundle — `NEXT_PUBLIC_*`):**
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_NAME` (default `"Photography Pixel Hub"`)
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` (default `http://localhost:3000`)
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL`
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`

**Runtime (server-only, never exposed to browser):**
- `DATABASE_URL`, `DIRECT_URL`
- `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`
- `R2_ENDPOINT`, `R2_ACCESS_KEY`, `R2_SECRET_KEY`, `R2_BUCKET`, `R2_PUBLIC_URL`
- `AUTH_SECRET`
- `NODE_ENV`

**Important caveat:** Although `lib/env.ts` validates all variables, most modules **bypass** the validated `env` object and read `process.env` directly (with `!` non-null assertions or `?? ""` fallbacks). The Zod validation only fires if something imports `lib/env.ts`. So a missing server var may NOT fail at startup unless `env.ts` is imported somewhere in the request path. `DIRECT_URL` is defined in the schema but was not referenced in any of the 13 files read — it's presumably consumed by `prisma.config.ts`/migrations (not in `src/`).

---

## 4. Styles — `src/styles/theme.css` and `src/styles/rtl.css`
Both exist and were read in full.

**`theme.css`** — CSS custom properties (light `:root` + `.dark`), using `oklch()` color space. Defines the full shadcn/ui token set: `--background`, `--foreground`, `--card`, `--popover`, `--primary` (blue `oklch(0.52 0.22 258)`), `--secondary`, `--muted`, `--accent`, `--destructive`, `--border`, `--input`, `--ring`, `--chart-1..5`, `--sidebar-*`, and `--radius: 0.625rem`. Branding = blue primary, "Photography Pixel Hub".

**`rtl.css`** — RTL support: `[dir="rtl"]` base right-align, `[dir="rtl"] [dir="ltr"]` LTR overrides, `.rtl-flip` (`scaleX(-1)`), `.rtl-space-reverse` sibling spacing, RTL scrollbar direction, and forces `direction: ltr` for `input[type="number"]`, `email`, `url`. (Note: there is also a third stylesheet `src/styles/dashboard.css` present, not requested/not read.)

---

## 5. Seed Script — `scripts/seed.ts`
**Exists** and read in full. Creates (via `upsert`):
1. Owner user — `owner@pixelpixel.hub` / `Owner@123` (bcrypt hashed, `UserRole.OWNER`)
2. Demo client — `demo@example.com`, token `demo-client-token-00000000`, `clientCode CL-000001`, status `ACTIVE`
3. Demo project — name "Demo Project", `projectCode PR-000001`, status `ProjectStatus.DRAFT`, `retentionPeriod TWENTY_FOUR_HOURS`, deadline `2026-12-31`, token `demo-project-token-00000000`

Notably, the seed script constructs its **own** `PrismaClient` with `PrismaPg` adapter directly (does NOT reuse the `globalThis` singleton from `src/lib/prisma/client.ts`), and disconnects in `finally`. Logs credentials to stdout.

---

## 6. `.env` / `.env.local` Existence
Confirmed via `Get-ChildItem -Force -Filter ".env*"`:
- `.env.example` — **exists** (2267 bytes). Contents read (see below).
- `.env.local` — **exists** (1708 bytes, modified 2026-07-19). Contents NOT read, per your instruction.
- Plain `.env` — **does NOT exist** (only `.env.example` and `.env.local` are present).

**`.env.example` contents** (full — contains real-looking credentials, including a Supabase DB password "Pixel Hub2026" and real JWT anon/service-role keys; R2/AUTH_SECRET are placeholders):
```
NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_NAME="Photography Pixel Hub"
NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL=https://your-domain.com
DATABASE_URL="postgresql://postgres.iplbqsuenmzqtlgwomiw:Pixel Hub2026@aws-0-eu-west-1.pooler.supabase.com:6543/postgres?pgbouncer=true"
DIRECT_URL="postgresql://postgres.iplbqsuenmzqtlgwomiw:Pixel Hub2026@aws-0-eu-west-1.pooler.supabase.com:5432/postgres"
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=https://iplbqsuenmzqtlgwomiw.supabase.co
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9... (real JWT)
SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9... (real JWT)
R2_ENDPOINT=https://[account-id].r2.cloudflarestorage.com
R2_ACCESS_KEY=[access-key-id]
R2_SECRET_KEY=[secret-access-key]
R2_BUCKET=[bucket-name]
R2_PUBLIC_URL=https://[custom-domain-or-r2-dev-url]
AUTH_SECRET=[generate-a-random-32-char-string]
NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL=http://localhost:3000   # duplicate, overrides earlier
NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_NAME=Photography Pixel Hub  # duplicate
```
⚠️ Note: `.env.example` contains **live secrets** (DB password + Supabase anon/service-role keys) committed to the repo, and `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL`/`NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_NAME` are declared twice (last wins → localhost). The anon key is public-by-design, but the **service-role key and DB password should not be in `.env.example`**.

---

## 7. `next-env.d.ts`
Glob `**/next-env.d.ts` found **no file at the project root** (1 match was ignored — almost certainly a generated copy under `.next/` or `node_modules/`). So there is **no `next-env.d.ts` at the workspace root**; it is either gitignored or not yet generated by `next dev`/`next build`.

---

## 8. Tailwind Config & Whether It's Actually Used
**`tailwind.config.ts`** (full contents):
```ts
const config: Config = {
  content: ["./src/**/*.{ts,tsx,mdx}"],
  theme: {
    extend: {
      fontFamily: {
        sans: ["var(--font-cairo)", "var(--font-inter)", "system-ui", "sans-serif"],
        latin: ["var(--font-inter)", "system-ui", "sans-serif"],
      },
      container: {
        center: true,
        padding: "1.5rem",
        screens: { "2xl": "1400px" },
      },
      breakpoints: {
        xs: "400px",
        "3xl": "1920px",
      },
    },
  },
  plugins: [],
};
```

### Usage analysis (searches run across `src/`)
| Config feature | Utility class searched | Used in codebase? | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| `fontFamily.sans` | `font-sans` | ✅ **YES** | `layout.tsx` body className `font-sans antialiased` |
| `fontFamily.latin` | `font-latin` | ❌ **NO** | Search `font-cairo\|font-inter\|font-latin` matched ONLY `layout.tsx` (and only on the `--font-cairo`/`--font-inter` CSS-variable definitions, not the `font-latin` utility). No component uses `font-latin`. |
| `container` | `container` class | ❌ **NO** | Search `\bcontainer\b` returned 4 hits, all false positives: `containerRef.current` (video-player.tsx) and a comment "bucket/container name" (storage/provider.ts). The Tailwind `container` utility is never applied. |
| `breakpoints.xs` (400px) | `xs:` | ❌ **NO** | Search `xs:\|3xl:` → **0 matches** in `src/`. |
| `breakpoints.3xl` (1920px) | `3xl:` | ❌ **NO** | Same search, 0 matches. |

**Notable bug:** The `breakpoints` key is defined under `theme.extend`, but **Tailwind reads breakpoint screens from `theme.screens`, not `theme.breakpoints`**. So `xs: "400px"` and `"3xl": "1920px"` are effectively **dead config** — they would never generate `xs:`/`3xl:` utilities even if someone tried to use them. (Standard Tailwind already provides `2xl` but not `xs`/`3xl`.) Combined with zero usage, this entire `breakpoints` block is inert.

**Bottom line:** Of the custom Tailwind config, only `fontFamily.sans` (via the `font-sans` class in the root layout) is actually exercised. `fontFamily.latin`, the `container` customization, and both custom `breakpoints` are defined but **never used** in the codebase (and `breakpoints` is additionally misconfigured for Tailwind).

---

## 1. Prisma Client Initialization
Two files in `src/lib/prisma/`:

**`src/lib/prisma/client.ts`** (full contents):
```ts
import { PrismaClient } from "@prisma/client";
import { PrismaPg } from "@prisma/adapter-pg";

const globalForPrisma = globalThis as unknown as {
  prisma: PrismaClient | undefined;
};

function createPrismaClient() {
  const adapter = new PrismaPg({
    connectionString: process.env.DATABASE_URL,
  });
  return new PrismaClient({ adapter });
}

export const prisma = globalForPrisma.prisma ?? createPrismaClient();

if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== "production") {
  globalForPrisma.prisma = prisma;
}
```
**`src/lib/prisma/index.ts`** (full contents):
```ts
export { prisma } from "./client";
```
**Key points:**
- Uses the **`@prisma/adapter-pg`** (`PrismaPg`) driver adapter — a direct Postgres connection via `DATABASE_URL` (NOT the default Prisma binary engine).

- Reads `process.env.DATABASE_URL` directly — does **not** use the validated `env` object from `src/lib/env.ts`.
- All other modules import via `@/lib/prisma` (the barrel `index.ts`).

## 8. Tailwind Config & Whether It's Actually Used
**`tailwind.config.ts`** (full contents):
```ts
const config: Config = {
  content: ["./src/**/*.{ts,tsx,mdx}"],
  theme: {
    extend: {
      fontFamily: {
        sans: ["var(--font-cairo)", "var(--font-inter)", "system-ui", "sans-serif"],
        latin: ["var(--font-inter)", "system-ui", "sans-serif"],
      },
      container: {
        center: true,
        padding: "1.5rem",
        screens: { "2xl": "1400px" },
      },
      breakpoints: {
        xs: "400px",
        "3xl": "1920px",
      },
    },
  },
  plugins: [],
};
```

### Usage analysis (searches run across `src/`)
| Config feature | Utility class searched | Used in codebase? | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| `fontFamily.sans` | `font-sans` | ✅ **YES** | `layout.tsx` body className `font-sans antialiased` |
| `fontFamily.latin` | `font-latin` | ❌ **NO** | Search `font-cairo\|font-inter\|font-latin` matched ONLY `layout.tsx` (and only on the `--font-cairo`/`--font-inter` CSS-variable definitions, not the `font-latin` utility). No component uses `font-latin`. |
| `container` | `container` class | ❌ **NO** | Search `\bcontainer\b` returned 4 hits, all false positives: `containerRef.current` (video-player.tsx) and a comment "bucket/container name" (storage/provider.ts). The Tailwind `container` utility is never applied. |
| `breakpoints.xs` (400px) | `xs:` | ❌ **NO** | Search `xs:\|3xl:` → **0 matches** in `src/`. |
| `breakpoints.3xl` (1920px) | `3xl:` | ❌ **NO** | Same search, 0 matches. |

**Notable bug:** The `breakpoints` key is defined under `theme.extend`, but **Tailwind reads breakpoint screens from `theme.screens`, not `theme.breakpoints`**. So `xs: "400px"` and `"3xl": "1920px"` are effectively **dead config** — they would never generate `xs:`/`3xl:` utilities even if someone tried to use them. (Standard Tailwind already provides `2xl` but not `xs`/`3xl`.) Combined with zero usage, this entire `breakpoints` block is inert.

**Bottom line:** Of the custom Tailwind config, only `fontFamily.sans` (via the `font-sans` class in the root layout) is actually exercised. `fontFamily.latin`, the `container` customization, and both custom `breakpoints` are defined but **never used** in the codebase (and `breakpoints` is additionally misconfigured for Tailwind).

AUTH_SECRET=[generate-a-random-32-char-string]
NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL=http://localhost:3000   # duplicate, overrides earlier
NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_NAME=Photography Pixel Hub  # duplicate
```
⚠️ Note: `.env.example` contains **live secrets** (DB password + Supabase anon/service-role keys) committed to the repo, and `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL`/`NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_NAME` are declared twice (last wins → localhost). The anon key is public-by-design, but the **service-role key and DB password should not be in `.env.example`**.

---

## 7. `next-env.d.ts`
Glob `**/next-env.d.ts` found **no file at the project root** (1 match was ignored — almost certainly a generated copy under `.next/` or `node_modules/`). So there is **no `next-env.d.ts` at the workspace root**; it is either gitignored or not yet generated by `next dev`/`next build`.

---

## 8. Tailwind Config & Whether It's Actually Used
**`tailwind.config.ts`** (full contents):
```ts
const config: Config = {
  content: ["./src/**/*.{ts,tsx,mdx}"],
  theme: {
    extend: {
      fontFamily: {
        sans: ["var(--font-cairo)", "var(--font-inter)", "system-ui", "sans-serif"],
        latin: ["var(--font-inter)", "system-ui", "sans-serif"],
      },
      container: {
        center: true,
        padding: "1.5rem",
        screens: { "2xl": "1400px" },
      },
      breakpoints: {
        xs: "400px",
        "3xl": "1920px",
      },
    },
  },
  plugins: [],
};
```

### Usage analysis (searches run across `src/`)
| Config feature | Utility class searched | Used in codebase? | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| `fontFamily.sans` | `font-sans` | ✅ **YES** | `layout.tsx` body className `font-sans antialiased` |
| `fontFamily.latin` | `font-latin` | ❌ **NO** | Search `font-cairo\|font-inter\|font-latin` matched ONLY `layout.tsx` (and only on the `--font-cairo`/`--font-inter` CSS-variable definitions, not the `font-latin` utility). No component uses `font-latin`. |
| `container` | `container` class | ❌ **NO** | Search `\bcontainer\b` returned 4 hits, all false positives: `containerRef.current` (video-player.tsx) and a comment "bucket/container name" (storage/provider.ts). The Tailwind `container` utility is never applied. |
| `breakpoints.xs` (400px) | `xs:` | ❌ **NO** | Search `xs:\|3xl:` → **0 matches** in `src/`. |
| `breakpoints.3xl` (1920px) | `3xl:` | ❌ **NO** | Same search, 0 matches. |

**Notable bug:** The `breakpoints` key is defined under `theme.extend`, but **Tailwind reads breakpoint screens from `theme.screens`, not `theme.breakpoints`**. So `xs: "400px"` and `"3xl": "1920px"` are effectively **dead config** — they would never generate `xs:`/`3xl:` utilities even if someone tried to use them. (Standard Tailwind already provides `2xl` but not `xs`/`3xl`.) Combined with zero usage, this entire `breakpoints` block is inert.

**Bottom line:** Of the custom Tailwind config, only `fontFamily.sans` (via the `font-sans` class in the root layout) is actually exercised. `fontFamily.latin`, the `container` customization, and both custom `breakpoints` are defined but **never used** in the codebase (and `breakpoints` is additionally misconfigured for Tailwind).

functionDeclarations.name: search_file_content
functionDeclarations.description: Searches for a regular expression pattern within the content of files in a specified directory, a specific file, or the current working directory. The search backend is ripgrep, so regex patterns use ripgrep/Rust regex syntax, not JavaScript RegExp syntax. If you need to search literal text that contains regex metacharacters (for example $, {, [, (, or \), set fixed_strings: true or -F: true instead of escaping by hand. Can filter files by a glob pattern when searching directories. By default, returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers; set output_mode to "files_with_matches" for just file paths or "count" for per-file match totals. Each matched line is truncated at 500 characters with a "[line truncated from N chars]" suffix to keep minified or base64 content from blowing up context.

pattern.description: The regular expression pattern to search for within file contents. This is interpreted by ripgrep/Rust regex, not JavaScript RegExp; avoid JS-only features such as lookbehind and backreferences. Regex syntax errors are returned by ripgrep. For literal text, especially strings containing regex metacharacters like $, {, [, (, or \, prefer fixed_strings: true or -F: true. When passing JSON args, escape only for JSON once: to match literal ${, use pattern "\\$\\{" (the regex engine sees \$\{), not "\\\\$\\\\{".

path.description: Optional: A path relative to the workspace root, or an absolute path to a directory or file to search within. If a file path is provided, searches only within that file. If a directory path is provided, searches all files in that directory. If omitted, searches the current working directory.

glob.description: Optional: A glob pattern to filter which files are searched (e.g., '*.js', '*.{ts,tsx}', 'src/**'). Single string only — combine multiple patterns via whitespace/comma separation (e.g. '*.js *.ts', '*.js,*.ts') or brace expansion ('*.{ts,tsx}'). Only applicable when searching directories, ignored for file searches. If omitted, searches all files (respecting potential global ignores).

output_mode.description: Optional: Output format. 'content' (default) returns matching lines with line numbers and optional context; 'files_with_matches' returns only the list of matching file paths; 'count' returns per-file match counts.

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of matches to return to prevent context overflow (default: 250, max: 1000). Values above 1000 are clamped automatically. Use lower values for broad searches, higher for specific searches. Pass 0 for unlimited.

head_limit.description: Optional: Claude Code aligned alias for 'maxResults'. When both are provided, head_limit wins. Pass 0 for unlimited (use sparingly — large result sets waste context).

offset.description: Optional: Number of matches to skip before returning results. Use this to continue after a truncated result's next offset.

-A.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -A.

-B.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -B.

-C.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before and after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -C.

context.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before and after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for -C.

-n.description: Optional: Whether to include line numbers in 'content' output. Defaults to true; set to false to omit them.

-i.description: Optional: Case-insensitive matching. Defaults to false (case-sensitive), matching ripgrep behavior.

fixed_strings.description: Optional: Treat the pattern as a literal string instead of a regular expression. Alias for -F.

-F.description: Optional: Treat the pattern as a literal string instead of a regular expression. Alias for fixed_strings.

type.description: Optional: ripgrep file type filter (rg --type). Common types: js, py, rust, go, java, etc. Accepts any alias from `rg --type-list`; the value is forwarded verbatim to ripgrep.

multiline.description: Optional: Enable multiline matching where patterns may span newlines (passes -U and --multiline-dotall to ripgrep). Only meaningful for 'content' output_mode. ^/$ anchors keep line boundaries behavior for portability across search backends.

include_hidden.description: Optional: Whether to descend into dot-prefixed directories (e.g. '.git/', '.codely-cli/') and match dot-prefixed files. Defaults to false to mirror ripgrep's default hidden-skip behaviour. Set to true to scan hidden paths explicitly (passes --hidden to ripgrep).
